---
title: "The Bitcoin Standard: An Economic Analysis"
description: "A research project examining Bitcoin as a potential alternative monetary system, presented at the Czech Secondary School Research Competition."
image: ../../assets/images/bitcoin-thumb-new.jpg
categories: [Economics, Research, Bitcoin]
---

## Overview

This project was my entry in the Secondary School Research Competition (Středoškolská Odborná Činnost) in 2022, in which I achieved 4th place in the Moravian regional round in the Economics and Management category.

The research examined the current financial system and its key challenges, particularly inflation and the Cantillon effect, and explored whether Bitcoin could serve as a viable alternative monetary standard. The paper analysed the properties of money, the mechanics of fiat currencies, and the economics of deflation before diving into Bitcoin's design and its potential to function as a basis for a national or global financial system.

As part of the qualitative research methodology, I conducted two in-depth interviews, each lasting 60-90 minutes, with a financial controller and analyst at a leading European fintech company, and with a cryptocurrency specialist. Their insights, alongside the views of prominent Czech economists, were used to form an independent conclusion on the feasibility of a Bitcoin-based monetary system.

The project was written in Czech and defended in front of an expert jury. Below you can watch the video presentation and read the full research paper.

---

## Presentation

{{< video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Q5pu7KkvZNk >}}

---

## Research Paper

<iframe src="../../assets/soc-paper.pdf" width="100%" height="700px" style="border: 1px solid rgba(0,0,0,0.1); border-radius: 0.5rem;"></iframe>
